library.description: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3: النوافذ، الإدخال، الأحداث، الصوت، والرسم الأساسي.

kind.description: استدعاءات تنفذ فعلاً، تغيّر حالة، أو تعيد نتيجة، أو تبني مورداً.

identity.name: SDL_GetCameraSupportedFormats
identity.titleArabic: جلب list native formats/sizes camera supports

summary.actualTranslationArabic: جلب list native formats/sizes camera supports
summary.actualMeaningArabic: جلب list native formats/sizes camera supports.
summary.purposeArabic: تستخدم هذه الدالة عندما تحتاج إلى جلب list native formats/sizes camera supports داخل التطبيق أو داخل مسار التنفيذ المرتبط بها.
summary.whyUseArabic: القيمة الحقيقية هنا هي الأثر التنفيذي الذي يحدثه الاستدعاء أو النتيجة التي يعيدها إلى التطبيق.
summary.carriedValueArabic:
القيمة العملية لهذا الاستدعاء تظهر في ناتجه ( SDL_CameraSpec **) Returns a NULL
terminated array of pointers to SDL_CameraSpec or NULL on failure; call
 SDL_GetError () for more information.
This is a single allocation that should be freed with SDL_free () when it is no longer needed. وفي التغيير الذي يحدثه على المورد أو الحالة المرتبطة به.
summary.sourceDescription: Get the list of native formats/sizes a camera supports.

parameters.name: instance_id
parameters.descriptionArabic: يمثل المعامل instance_id من النوع SDL_CameraID القيمة أو المورد الذي يعتمد عليه هذا الاستدعاء.

parameters.name: count
parameters.descriptionArabic: يمثل المعامل count من النوع int * القيمة أو المورد الذي يعتمد عليه هذا الاستدعاء.
parameters.originalDescription:
a pointer filled in with the number of elements in the list, may be
NULL.

remarks:
This returns a list of all formats and frame sizes that a specific
camera can offer. This is useful if your app can accept a variety of
image formats and sizes and so want to find the optimal spec that
doesn't require conversion.

This function isn't strictly required; if you call SDL_OpenCamera with a NULL spec, SDL will
choose a native format for you, and if you instead specify a desired
format, it will transparently convert to the requested format on your
behalf.

If count is not NULL, it will be filled with the number
of elements in the returned array.

Note that it's legal for a camera to supply an empty list. This is
what will happen on Emscripten builds, since that platform won't tell
 anything about available cameras until you've opened one, and
won't even tell if there is a camera until the user has given
you permission to check through a scary warning popup.
details.returns:
( SDL_CameraSpec **) Returns a NULL
terminated array of pointers to SDL_CameraSpec or NULL on failure; call
 SDL_GetError () for more information.
This is a single allocation that should be freed with SDL_free () when it is no longer needed.
details.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
